units.content: the actor in claim 1 is being specified as one or more of several possible types
a human operator receives the feedback signal through a human-machine interface

the human operator modifies at least one of: sampling schedule, sensor-selection parameter, or data-gating parameter of the physical measurement apparatus, as a result of consuming the feedback signal
a human operator receives the feedback signal (without a specified interface constraint)
the human operator annotates the storage with an additional directed entry, as a result of consuming the feedback signal
an automated control subsystem receives and acts on the feedback signal without any human intermediation
the claim treats human-mediated and fully automated consumption of the feedback signal as legally distinct and independently claimable actor types
the actor role in claim 1 is intentionally broad, covering the full spectrum from direct human control to full automation, with the feedback signal as the common operative element
